zh.lifeSummary: Marguerite Sibylle de Courtenay於大約1194年出生在法國哈帕格-亞登芒內馬雷易爾--布利。她和Raoul I of Lusignan至少有2個女兒。她去世於1270年7月17日在德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿維利德爾瑙馬利塔爾享年77歲，埋葬在德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿維利德爾瑙馬利塔爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Marguerite Sibylle de Courtenay于大约1194年出生在法国哈帕格-亞登芒內馬雷易爾--布利。她和Raoul I of Lusignan至少有2个女儿。她去世于1270年7月17日在德国海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿維利德爾瑙馬利塔爾享年77岁，埋葬在德国海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿維利德爾瑙馬利塔爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Courtenay near Sens in northern France, or some other place similarly named, from the name of a Gallo-Roman landlord, Curtenus (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish: English surname adopted for Ó Curnáin ‘descendant of Curnán’, an Old Irish personal name based on a diminutive of corn ‘horn’.

History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Athon de Courtenay, who lived in Courtenay in Loiret in the early 11th century. Renaud de Courtenay held land in Devon in the 12th century, and the family later held the title of Earls of Devon following the marriage of Hugh de Courtenay to Margaret de Bohun, granddaughter of King Edward I, in 1325.

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

nameMeanings.name: Sibylle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alain, Etienne, Jean-Michael.
French: from the female personal name Sibille, from Greek Sibylla (see Sibley ).

en.lifeSummary: Marguerite Sibylle de Courtenay was born about 1194, in Mareuil-en-Brie, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, France. She had at least 2 daughters with Raoul I of Lusignan. She died on 17 July 1270, in Marienthal, Dernau, Ahrweiler, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, at the age of 77, and was buried in Marienthal, Dernau, Ahrweiler, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Marguerite Sibylle de Courtenay est née environ 1194, à Mareuil-en-Brie, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, France. Elle a eu au moins 2 filles avec Raoul I of Lusignan. Elle est décédée le 17 juillet 1270, à Marienthal, Dernau, Landkreis Ahrweiler, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Marienthal, Dernau, Landkreis Ahrweiler, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne.

Sie starb am 17. Juli 1270 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Marienthal, Dernau, Landkreis Ahrweiler, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland und wurde in Marienthal, Dernau, Landkreis Ahrweiler, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Marguerite Sibylle de Courtenay(은)는 대략 1194년 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 마니 마우이--브리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Raoul I of Lusignan(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1270년 7월 17일 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 랜드크레이스 아위러 더나 마앵솔에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 랜드크레이스 아위러 더나 마앵솔에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Marguerite Sibylle de Courtenay nasceu aproximadamente 1194, em Mareuil-en-Brie, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, França. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Raoul I of Lusignan. Ela faleceu em 17 de julho de 1270, em Marienthal, Dernau, Landkreis Ahrweiler, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha, com 77 anos, e foi sepultada em Marienthal, Dernau, Landkreis Ahrweiler, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Marguerite Sibylle de Courtenay родилась около 1194 г. в местности мареуил-ен-бри, марн, чампагне-арденн, Франция. У неё было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Raoul I of Lusignan. Она умерла 17 июля 1270 г. в местности мариентэл, дирнау, ландкрис ахрвилир, раинланд-пфалз, Германия в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности мариентэл, дирнау, ландкрис ахрвилир, раинланд-пфалз, Германия.
